2025-08-01.title: Cécile Dionne, Who Found Fame and Despair as a Quintuplet, Dies at 91

2025-08-25.title: Typhoon Slams Central Vietnam as Fear of Flooding Intensifies
2025-08-25.abstract: More than 300,000 people have been told to evacuate as Typhoon Kajiki moved across a wide area between Hanoi and Da Nang.

2025-08-26.title: Too Old, Too Uneducated: Aging Workers in Beijing Struggle for Work
2025-08-26.abstract: China’s economic slowdown has fallen especially hard on older migrant workers, who often don’t have the technical skills that employers are seeking.

2025-08-26.title: Australia Says Iran Was Behind Antisemitic Arson Attacks on Its Soil
2025-08-26.abstract: The government expelled Iran’s diplomats, saying the country had orchestrated attacks on a Melbourne synagogue and a Sydney restaurant.

2025-08-26.title: Tuesday Briefing: Israeli Strikes Killed Five Journalists
2025-08-26.abstract: Also, a knockoff Starbucks in North Korea.

2025-08-26.title: 2 Police Officers Killed in Shooting in Rural Australia
2025-08-26.abstract: A third officer was wounded. Local authorities urged residents to avoid the area, about 200 miles from Melbourne, as they searched for the gunman.

2025-08-26.title: A Peace Deal for Ukraine Could Test German Reluctance to Deploy Troops
2025-08-26.abstract: The question of whether to send soldiers to a postwar Ukraine is the latest chapter in an evolving relationship between Germans and their military.

2025-08-27.title: British Summers Are Getting Hotter. So Are Houses.
2025-08-27.abstract: Built for a cooler climate, many homes need to be retrofitted for warming temperatures. It won’t be as simple as installing air-conditioning.

2025-08-27.title: Targeted by U.S. Ally, an Arab Dissident Vanished Across Borders
2025-08-27.abstract: After criticizing the United Arab Emirates on social media, an Egyptian activist was extradited to the Gulf country, where he has been detained without trial for months.

2025-08-27.title: Germany Proposes Plan to Boost Recruitment or Resort to Draft
2025-08-27.abstract: Some detractors think the new bill is too weak to fill the recruitment gap. Others worry it will inevitably lead to forced conscription.

2025-08-27.title: Denmark Summons U.S. Envoy Over a Reported ‘Influence Operation’ in Greenland
2025-08-27.abstract: It was the latest twist in a simmering diplomatic conflict over Greenland. President Trump wants the island to be a U.S. territory, but Denmark refuses to give it up.

2025-08-27.title: Floods and Landslides Kill Dozens on Pilgrimage Route in Kashmir
2025-08-27.abstract: Rescue workers pulled bodies from the debris after rain sent boulders and mud crashing down.

2025-08-28.title: Jimmy Lai’s Freedom May Now Hinge on Beijing and Trump
2025-08-28.abstract: As the outspoken Hong Kong publisher awaits a verdict, his trial has become a test of China’s resolve to crush dissent, and of whether President Trump can free him.

2025-08-28.title: What to Know About Jimmy Lai’s National Security Trial in Hong Kong
2025-08-28.abstract: The media tycoon, jailed since 2020, ran a now-defunct newspaper that was critical of the government. A court has issued a guilty verdict in his case and he could face life in prison.

2025-08-28.title: Austria’s Hills Are Still Alive, 60 Years Later
2025-08-28.abstract: In Salzburg, an anniversary of “The Sound of Music” looks grand through a child’s eyes, even if the locals are gazing elsewhere.

2025-08-28.title: Trump Tries to Derail Climate Progress
2025-08-28.abstract: Also, the film festival season kicks off.

2025-08-28.title: Kim Jong-un and Putin Top List of U.S. Rivals Convening in Beijing Next Week
2025-08-28.abstract: They will join China’s leader, Xi Jinping, and about 20 other heads of state, some of whom share a strong distrust of the United States.

2025-08-29.title: Why Thailand’s Politics Are in Constant Crisis
2025-08-29.abstract: While the country is a constitutional monarchy that holds regular elections, analysts say it is beholden to an unelected old guard.

2025-08-29.title: China Is Trying to Expand Its Social Safety Net. Yet Many Chinese Are Worried.
2025-08-29.abstract: A move to force employers to pay into benefits for their employees has left people worrying that small businesses will close and jobs will be lost.

2025-08-29.title: August in Paris: When the City Empties Out, Locals Left Behind Rejoice
2025-08-29.abstract: With many away for long vacations, the capital is transformed into a sleepy, roomy, more friendly place.

2025-08-29.title: Russia’s Deadly Attack on Kyiv
2025-08-29.abstract: Also, the secret of “KPop Demon Hunters.”

2025-08-29.title: Protests Spread Across Indonesia After a Deadly Clash With Police
2025-08-29.abstract: Rising unemployment and inflation have driven days of demonstrations. A 21-year-old man was killed when police responded with force to a protest on Thursday.

2025-08-30.title: London’s Peace Monk Chants, Drums and Walks to Urge an End to War
2025-08-30.abstract: For 40 years, the Rev. Gyoro Nagase has overseen a temple to peace in a popular park in Battersea, his ever-present drum by his side.

2025-08-30.title: An ‘Economic Storm’ of Crises Is Battering Afghanistan
2025-08-30.abstract: Afghanistan was on a timid recovery path. But four years after the Taliban retook power, it has been badly hit by aid cuts and an inflow of two million Afghans forced out of Iran and Pakistan.

2025-08-30.title: China Rekindles Wartime Fury, Stirring Fears of Anti-Japan Hate
2025-08-30.abstract: A series of World War II dramas about China’s fight against Japan is drawing audiences to their feet, and, in some cases, to tears. Some say it helps deflect public discontent.

2025-08-30.title: Four Dead in Indonesia Unrest, Official Says
2025-08-30.abstract: In spreading protests against the president, three government workers died after jumping from a building that protesters set afire. A fourth person was attacked by a crowd and later died of his injuries.

2025-08-30.title: Croquet, Anyone? Making Moscow a Vast Fun Zone to Divert Minds From War.
2025-08-30.abstract: A festival known as Summer in Moscow showcases the city’s transformation into an ultramodern metropolis. But the feast can’t last forever.

2025-08-31.title: A City Reinvented: Paris Is Now Greater Paris
2025-08-31.abstract: The periphery of the French capital is more vital than ever, and its creativity and dynamism have blurred old boundaries.

2025-08-31.title: Indonesia’s Leader Says He Will Bow to Some Protester Demands After Riots
2025-08-31.abstract: President Prabowo Subianto of Indonesia acknowledged the concerns of the protesters and said his government would make concessions.

2025-08-31.title: Russian Strikes on Western Assets in Ukraine Send an Ominous Message
2025-08-31.abstract: Hitting an American-run factory and European diplomatic offices, the Kremlin appeared to signal that it would resist Western efforts to make peace and protect Ukraine, analysts and officials said.

2025-08-31.title: A Shattered City Asks for Its Sacrifice to Be Repaid
2025-08-31.abstract: Millions of Syrians from destroyed places like Daraya feel they did everything possible to overthrow the Assad government, but they still await reconstruction aid.

2025-08-31.title: Deadly Floods in Punjab Devastate Pakistan’s Breadbasket
2025-08-31.abstract: Housing communities and businesses that rely on agriculture have been destroyed in the country’s largest province.
